rationale.R1.2: The section makes several mathematical claims that are in tension: (1) the problem is framed as 'non-convex' but the proven convergence uses a relaxed convex simplex, not the hard-selection vertex set — the gap between these is never bridged; (2) the section simultaneously asserts L-smooth + μ-strongly convex structure and O(1/√T) rates — strong convexity normally yields O(1/T) or faster, so citing O(1/√T) as a primary rate here is inconsistent unless the strong-convexity claim applies only to the relaxed interior and the bandit rate governs the outer problem; (3) the concluding claim that hard selection 'imposes no additional rate penalties' is asserted without proof in this section. Logical consistency is load-bearing.
rationale.R1.3: The stated O(log K / √T) result follows from mirror descent on the relaxed simplex under specific conditions (bounded φ_i, twice-differentiable ℓ with bounded Hessian), but the headline O(1/√T) claim for 'hard selection' is not evidently derived from those same conditions — the correspondence must be checked. The empirical validation is deferred to Section 5 without a cross-section claim being made precise here.
rationale.R1.4: The 'first O(1/√T) rate for selection aggregators in this regime' is a strong priority claim. It warrants verification against the cited Cesa-Bianchi & Lugosi (2006) and Bubeck (2015) to confirm neither already establishes this or a tighter bound in the same setting.
rationale.R1.7: Two specific bibliographic claims are made: the O(log K / √T) rate 'matches the optimal rate for online learning over the simplex (Cesa-Bianchi and Lugosi, 2006)' and entropy-regularized mirror descent rates are recovered from 'Bubeck (2015).' Both attributions must be verified for accuracy and correct theorem correspondence.
